contentBlocks.original:
मेघश्यामं पीतकौशेयवासं श्रीवत्साङ्कं कौस्तुभोद्भासिताङ्गम् ।
पुण्योपेतं पुण्डरीकायताक्षं विष्णुं वन्दे सर्वलोकैकनाथम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
meghaśyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsaṃ śrīvatsāṅkaṃ kaustubhodbhāsitāṅgam .
puṇyopetaṃ puṇḍarīkāyatākṣaṃ viṣṇuṃ vande sarvalokaikanātham ..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Vishnu who is beautiful like the dark clouds, and who is wearing yellow garments of silk; Who has the mark of Srivatsa on his chest; and whose body is shining with the radiance of the kaushtubha mani, whose form is permeated with holiness, and whose beautiful eyes are extended like the lotus petals; We salute Sri Vishnu who is the one lord of all the lokas.
contentBlocks.meanings: मेघश्यामं (meghaśyāmam) – dark as a cloud (megha:cloud | śyāmam:dark); पीतकौशेयवासं (pītakauśeyavāsam) – wearing yellow silk (pīta:yellow | kauśeya:silk | vāsam:wearing); श्रीवत्साङ्कं (śrīvatsāṅkam) – marked with Śrīvatsa (śrīvatsa:Śrīvatsa | aṅkam:marked); कौस्तुभोद्भासिताङ्गम् (kaustubhodbhāsitāṅgam) – body shining with Kaustubha (kaustubha:Kaustubha | udbhāsita:shining | aṅgam:body); पुण्योपेतं (puṇyopetam) – endowed with holiness (puṇya:holiness | upetam:endowed with); पुण्डरीकायताक्षं (puṇḍarīkāyatākṣam) – lotus-like eyes (puṇḍarīka:lotus | āyata:extended | akṣam:eyes); विष्णुं (viṣṇum) – Vishnu; वन्दे (vande) – I salute; सर्वलोकैकनाथम् (sarvalokaikanātham) – one lord of all worlds (sarva:all | loka:worlds | eka:one | nātham:lord);
